results.rendered_html:
1. abhi-√yā to go up to in a hostile manner, attack, assail,  RV. i, 174, 5  (aor. Subj. -yāsiṣat),  AV.  &c.;
to go up to, approach, obtain,  MBh.  &c.;
to devote one's self to take up (as pāṣaṇḍam, ‘heterodoxies’),  BhP.  :
Caus. to cause to approach, send towards,  BhP.

results.rendered_html:
abhiyā 2 P.
1 To go up to, approach, go or repair to; abhiyayau sa himācalamucchritaṃ  Ki. 5. 1 ;  R. 9. 27. 
2 To come or draw near (without an object); come (as time).
3 To march against, attack, assail, encounter; kuberādabhiyāsyamānāt  R. 5. 30 ;  Dk. 30. 
4 To give or resign oneself to, devote, attach or betake oneself to.
(pb)
5 To partake or share in, get, obtain.
— Caus. To send away.
